problem: After publishing, concurrent updates could corrupt snapshot. State machine must enforce PUBLISHED → ARCHIVED immutability.
solution: T633 pins schemaVersion at draft creation. Published version becomes append-only. No update path post-publish.
tradeoff: Immutability guarantees no post-publish edits, but forces version branching for corrections.

problem: Content approval requires sequential validation. Parallel assignment could violate audit ordering.
solution: T634 orchestrates 4 sequential stages with role-based gates. Each stage queries FREEDOM role-map.
tradeoff: Sequential stages add latency, but guarantee audit completeness.

problem: Scheduled publish must survive failures without publishing twice.
solution: T635 uses SETNX lock per schedule: SETNX(publish-schedule:{contentId}:{timestamp}). On hold, return cached result.
tradeoff: SETNX adds Redis dependency, but guarantees exactly-once semantics.

topic: Append-Only Content Metrics with PII Masking
problem: Analytics must track counters without leaking PII. GDPR deletion must clear PII but preserve counts.
solution: T636 appends records with piiScrubbed flag. Deletion flips flag, never deletes.
tradeoff: Append-only prevents in-place fixes, but guarantees audit immutability.

problem: Schema updates could break validation. Only additive changes permitted.
solution: T633 enforces ISchemaAdditiveValidator: only new optional fields allowed.
tradeoff: Limited expressiveness, but zero re-validation failures.

problem: Multi-tenant ops must respect tenant boundaries. Hardcoded tenantId risks cross-tenant leakage.
solution: DNA-5: Fabric methods read tenantId from ALS internally. Services call getTenantId() only for validation.
tradeoff: ALS adds implicit context complexity, but eliminates surface-level tenantId bugs.
